description:
A conceptual diagram illustrating a 3D stack of 6x6 grid cells with an associated mathematical formula. The diagram shows five layers of grids, each 6 cells wide and 6 cells high. In the frontmost layer, a central cell is highlighted in dark green and labelled `Pc`. Cells immediately adjacent to `Pc`, forming a cross pattern (one cell above, one below, one to the left, and one to the right of `Pc`), are coloured light green. All other visible cells in the frontmost layer are orange. In the layers behind, the cells that align with the light green cross pattern of the front layer are also light green, while the remaining cells are orange. One cell in the upper right quadrant of the frontmost layer is labelled `Pi`.

To the right of the grid, a formula for `SCR` is provided:
`SCR = A^2(Pc) / (1/NP * Σ(i ∈ Red) A^2(Pi))`
Where:
- `A(Pc)` represents the amplitude or area of the central cell `Pc`.
- `A(Pi)` represents the amplitude or area of the i-th surrounding cell `Pi`.
- `NP` is the number of cells in the region designated as "Red".
- `Σ(i ∈ Red)` indicates the summation over all cells `i` that are part of the "Red" region. The "Red" region is not explicitly defined by colour in the diagram, but typically refers to the surrounding, non-central cells.
